title: Run record (v1)
description: Append-only, citable record of one (solver, instance, parameters, hardware) run. The repro_hash deterministically identifies the inputs; two runs with the same hash on the same code_version are expected to produce identical results modulo seeded randomness.

record_id.description: Sortable, unique identifier (timestamp + short random suffix).

code_version.description: Git SHA at run time, or '<package-version>+dev' when not in a git tree.

solver.description: Solver identity at run time (a copy of the registry entry, frozen for reproducibility).

parameters.description: Full set of sample() keyword arguments used for this run, including seed and any kernel-mode flags.

hardware_id.description: Concrete hardware identifier observed at run time (may be more specific than solver.hardware).

repro_hash.description: Deterministic hash over (code_version, instance_id, solver identity, normalized parameters).

results.description: Summary of the run (best energy, feasibility counts, timing, honest convergence flag).

num_feasible.description: Number of CQM-feasible samples; null when the sampler doesn't expose feasibility (e.g. raw BQM samplers).

expected_optimum.description: Known optimum (or best-known value) the run aimed at, in user-facing units. Null when the instance has no ground truth.

gap_to_expected.description: Absolute gap between best_user_energy and expected_optimum. Null when either is missing.

converged_to_expected.description: True iff gap_to_expected is within max(1e-3 * |expected|, 1e-9). Null when the instance has no ground truth — the dashboard must render that as 'heuristic estimate, no ground truth available.'

sample_set_path.description: Relative path under benchmarks/archive to a gzipped SampleSet serialization, or null when no sample-level archive was kept.
